evals.prompt: Client's 2024 Form 1040 expected to show $40K balance due. Today is April 10, 2025. He wants to file Form 4868 extension to October 15. Will the extension extend his time to PAY?
evals.expected_output: Predicts NO with HIGH confidence — Form 4868 extends only the FILING deadline, not the PAYMENT deadline. Tax must be paid by April 15, 2025 to avoid §6651(a)(2) failure-to-pay penalty (0.5% per month, max 25%) and §6601 interest. Recommend: (1) File Form 4868 by April 15; (2) Pay the $40K estimated balance with the extension via direct debit / EFTPS / check; (3) If unable to pay full amount, pay what is possible and consider installment agreement (Form 9465) to manage the unpaid balance. Cite §6151, §6081, §6651(a)(2), §6601.
expectations: Distinguishes filing extension from payment extension
Recommends Form 9465 if unable to pay

evals.prompt: Single taxpayer, 2024 AGI $200K. Wants to use 110% safe harbor for 2025 estimated tax. 2024 total tax was $48,000. What's the Q1 2025 estimated payment?
evals.expected_output: Computes 110% × $48,000 = $52,800 annual safe-harbor target; divided by 4 quarters = $13,200 per quarterly payment. §6654(d)(1)(B)(ii) — 110% of prior-year tax safe harbor applies because AGI > $150K. Q1 2025 deadline: April 15, 2025 (per §6654(c)). Recommend $13,200 paid via Form 1040-ES voucher, EFTPS, or IRS Direct Pay by April 15, 2025. Note: any withholding from W-2 / 1099-R reduces the required estimated payment; §6654(g) treats withholding as paid evenly across quarters.

evals.prompt: Decedent died on March 15, 2024 with $7M estate (under threshold). Spouse wants to file Form 706 for portability under Rev. Proc. 2022-32. What's the deadline?
evals.expected_output: Computes that under Rev. Proc. 2022-32, an estate not required to file Form 706 (under threshold) has 5 years from date of death to file for portability — i.e., March 15, 2029. The standard 9-month §6075(a) deadline (December 15, 2024) does NOT apply because the estate was below threshold; Rev. Proc. 2022-32 extends automatically. Recommend filing within the 5-year window with notation 'FILED PURSUANT TO REV. PROC. 2022-32 TO ELECT PORTABILITY' on the top of Form 706. Cite §6075(a), Rev. Proc. 2022-32.
